hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 8, 3, Queen. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.

wrongReasons: J 2 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with J 2.
9 3 makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
J 5 has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with J 5.
hand.explanation:
Answer: A 10

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
